title: switch gpt-image-2 to openai direct, drop azure round-robin
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Direct line to the source
gist.blurb: We've rewired gpt-image-2 to talk directly to OpenAI, dropping the old multi-region setup. This means higher rate limits and smoother, faster generations for everyone.
gist.summary: Switched the gpt-image-2 model backend from an Azure round-robin setup to a direct OpenAI API integration.
gist.impact: Users will experience significantly higher generation capacity and fewer rate limits when using the gpt-image-2 model.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a network engineer, bypassing a tangled mess of old cables (Azure round-robin) to plug a single, massive, glowing green cable directly into a sleek server (OpenAI direct). Represents simplification and higher capacity.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny yellow hardhat, plugging a massive glowing lime green (#ecf874) cable directly into a sleek retro server mainframe. A tangled mess of old cables sits discarded in a box nearby. Soft pastel gradient background with warm lighting. Chunky retro sprites, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
